text: No - inline content is not the adoption lever. With the image returned inline vs path-only, voluntary (optional-mode) adoption is 0/36 in both arms; forcing reaches ~31-34/36 regardless of format. The path-not-content friction is not the barrier - the agent does not choose visualization even when the picture is handed to it directly. This refutes H7a, the paper's proposed constructive lever.

comments.discrepancy: The paper's current draft thesis names inline-content return as the constructive adoption lever, but this study's own data refutes it for voluntary adoption. Recorded faithfully here; the paper write-up lags the data.
